Tests the behavior of the Math.pow function with negative infinity as an exponent.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of JavaScript's `Math.pow` function when used with negative infinity as the exponent. It verifies that dividing 1 by `Math.pow(5, -Infinity)` results in `Infinity`. This test ensures that the mathematical operation involving exponentiation with negative infinity behaves as expected according to JavaScript's numerical computation rules. The code is narrow in functionality, focusing on a specific mathematical operation and its expected result.
